name: Grape Jelly
recipeInstructions:
1. Wash the grapes and place them in a large kettle. Mash them slightly.
2. Wash the apple and cut it into slices without coring or seeding. Add the apple slices to the grapes along with 1/2 cup of water.
3. Bring the mixture to a boil and cook until the grapes soften, about ten minutes.
4. Drain through a damp jelly bag without pressing the pulp. Refrigerate overnight.
5. Strain again to remove tartrate crystals.
6. Measure the juice into a large kettle. For every cup of juice, add 3/4 cup sugar (about four cups of juice total).
7. Bring to a boil over medium-high heat and cook until the mixture reaches jelly stage at 220 degrees F on a candy thermometer.
8. Skim foam from the surface and pour the jelly into sterilized half-pint glasses. Seal with paraffin.
